description: Edit properties across multiple notes at once. Use a checkbox property to mark notes for updating. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Address Obsidian plugin review feedback in https://github.com/gtritchie/bulk-properties/pull/51
* Attest release artifacts with build provenance in https://github.com/gtritchie/bulk-properties/pull/52


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gtritchie/bulk-properties/compare/1.0.8...1.0.9

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Enhancements
- **Delete selected button relocated** — The delete button now sits at the left of the select/deselect row with neutral styling, giving Update properties a clearer place as the primary action.

## Bug Fixes
- **Pop-out window reliability** — Timers and selection interactions in the bulk edit modal now stay tied to the window that opened them, so the modal behaves correctly when used in a pop-out window.

## Other
- **Minimum Obsidian version is now 1.8.7** — The supported floor has been raised to match the Obsidian APIs the plugin actually uses.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Enhancements
- **Bulk edit modal spacing** — The new value region now has extra breathing room above the next setting's divider line for a cleaner layout.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features
- **Large operation warning** — After bulk operations that modify more than 25 notes, a modal warns that Obsidian's metadata cache may take several minutes to re-index, with an option to suppress future warnings.

## Enhancements
- **Faster bulk operations** — Bulk updates, deselects, and property removals now run in parallel, making them meaningfully faster on large batches. Deletes still run one at a time so cancellation stays responsive.

## Other
- **UI text uses "notes" instead of "files"** — All user-visible text now says "notes" to match Obsidian's style guide. Command IDs and hotkey bindings are unchanged.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features
- **Delete selected files** — The bulk edit dialog now has a "Delete selected files" button that moves files to the location configured in Obsidian's "Files & links → Deleted files" setting.
## Enhancements
- **Clickable file rows** — Clicking anywhere on a file path in the bulk edit dialog now toggles its checkbox, not just the checkbox itself.
- **Link to plugin settings from empty state** — When no properties are configured, the empty-state message in the bulk edit dialog now includes a clickable link that opens the plugin settings directly.
- **Warning for wrong selection property type** — Plugin settings now warn you when the selection property is set to something other than a Checkbox type.
- **Prevent selection property conflicts** — The selection property can no longer be set to a name that matches one of your configured edit properties, with a warning shown if a pre-existing conflict is detected.
## Bug Fixes
- **Type dropdown no longer auto-selects Text** — When adding a property name that doesn't exist in your vault, the type dropdown now stays on "Choose type…" instead of silently defaulting to Text.
- **List properties keep duplicate values on merge** — Merging a value into a list property no longer drops existing duplicate entries.
- **Empty list properties now accept merged values** — Files with an empty list property (e.g. `items:` with no value) now correctly receive merged values instead of being skipped.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes
- **Selection property not saved from dropdown** — Choosing a property name from the autocomplete suggestion list in settings appeared to work but didn't actually save the change.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features
- **Merge, replace, and delete actions for multi-value properties** — When editing tags, aliases, or list properties, you can now choose to merge new values into existing ones, replace all values, or delete specific values.

## Enhancements
- **Pill-style input with autocomplete for multi-value properties** — Tags, aliases, and list properties now use a pill/chip input matching Obsidian's native editor, with autocomplete suggestions drawn from existing values in your vault.
- **Friendly type names in settings** — The property type dropdown and list now show readable names like "Date & time" and "List" instead of internal identifiers.
- **Smarter property name suggestions** — Properties already in your configuration and the selection property are filtered out of the autocomplete dropdown when adding a new property.

## Bug Fixes
- **Invalid input no longer silently discarded** — Previously, entering an invalid tag name would quietly drop the text. Now the input is preserved in the field with a notice explaining what's wrong, and the Update button is disabled until the issue is resolved.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

* Added confirmation dialog for "Deselect all files"
* Improved performance in large vaults when status bar is visible
* Fixed a rare issue where rapid checkbox toggling in the bulk edit dialog could cause selection state to get out of sync
